response.justification: Maria Rodriguez brings over 13 years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and distributed systems, with solid fundamentals in TDD and software craftsmanship. However, the role demands deep expertise in experimentation platforms, telemetry infrastructure, feature flagging, growth measurement, and statistical analysis — none of which appear anywhere in the resume. The candidate's strongest distributed-systems signal (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby) is high-throughput data integration, not experimentation or growth. The current title is Staff Software Engineer, and there is no evidence of the cross-team architectural leadership or IC6-scope ownership expected at the Principal level. The gap between the candidate's profile and the core role requirements is too wide to bridge in an interview.
strengths: 13+ years of continuous engineering experience across high-scale distributed systems, embedded, and full-stack domains demonstrates broad technical range
Hands-on exposure to high-throughput, low-latency infrastructure at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP req/s) shows comfort with production systems at scale
Strong polyglot background (C, C++, TypeScript, Java, DLang, Swift) and consistent craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) align with code-quality expectations
concerns: Zero evidence of experimentation platform work — no A/B testing, feature flagging, experiment assignment, rollout automation, or bias/guardrail systems anywhere in the resume
No telemetry, metrics instrumentation, or data analysis experience; growth measurement and statistical methods are core to this role and completely absent
No demonstrated Principal/IC6-level scope: cross-team technical leadership, multi-workstream ownership, or architectural decision-making at org scale are not evidenced

key_factors.factor: No experimentation or growth engineering experience
No telemetry or metrics infrastructure background
High-scale distributed systems exposure at Sociomantic
